access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q34.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn A, bài viết nêu: "When the Soviet Union successfully launched Sputnik 1 in 1957, it was the first artificial satellite ever to enter orbit around the Earth, and the project's success marked a significant milestone in the history of the exploration of space. There was suddenly a profound change in human perspective, a change which would, in subsequent years, become ever more marked as scientists progressively acquired the ability to study aspects of the planet from a new (and not Earth-bound) point of view. However, the technology employed on that first satellite mission was astoundingly simple when compared to what would follow.". Cụm B khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q34.evidence: When the Soviet Union successfully launched Sputnik 1 in 1957, it was the first artificial satellite ever to enter orbit around the Earth, and the project's success marked a significant milestone in the history of the exploration of space. There was suddenly a profound change in human perspective, a change which would, in subsequent years, become ever more marked as scientists progressively acquired the ability to study aspects of the planet from a new (and not Earth-bound) point of view. However, the technology employed on that first satellite mission was astoundingly simple when compared to what would follow._

q39.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn E, bài viết nêu: "With more satellites and spacecraft being launched every year, the problem of space debris needs a solution, one of which was trialled by the Japanese, who took Kyoritsu into orbit and released it. Kyoritsu is a type of satellite from which a kilometre-long cable manufactured by a fishing net company is tethered to a 20-kilogram mass. As this moves through the Earth’s magnetic field, it generates an electric current which slows down space debris it comes into contact with.". Cụm A khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q39.evidence: With more satellites and spacecraft being launched every year, the problem of space debris needs a solution, one of which was trialled by the Japanese, who took Kyoritsu into orbit and released it. As this moves through the Earth’s magnetic field, it generates an electric current which slows down space debris it comes into contact with._
